items.q: How is this different from a normal agency?
items.a: Most agencies sell services — websites, ads, SEO. We sell outcomes: more revenue, lower costs, faster operations. Every project is scoped with success metrics before we write a line of code, and we measure ourselves against them every month.
Single packages start at ₹60K (RevSite Pro). Retainers start at ₹30K/month. Our most common engagement is a phased build that pays for itself in the first 60–90 days.

items.q: How long until we see results?
items.a: Conversion improvements show up in week 1 of going live. WhatsApp automation pays back in week 2. SEO compounds over 4–6 months. ERP rollouts deliver visible ROI in the first 30 days.
Yes. Our GlobalScale Suite is built specifically for brands selling to NRIs and global markets. We've shipped multi-language, multi-currency systems for clients in the US, UAE, and Southeast Asia.

items.q: Can we start with just one system?
items.a: Absolutely. The fastest ROI usually comes from one of three: a website rebuild, WhatsApp automation, or local SEO. We pick the one that pays back the fastest for your specific business.

items.q: What does the discovery look like?
items.a: A paid 3-hour session. We map your funnel, identify revenue leaks, and deliver a written blueprint with scope, timeline, and projected outcomes. The fee is offset against any project you sign.

home.title: What is a revenue system (and why it beats a website)?
home.summary: A revenue system is the full stack that turns attention into money — site, capture, nurture, convert, retain. It's the replacement for the fragmented website + ads + spreadsheet setup most SMEs use.
body: A traditional agency delivers a website. A revenue system agency delivers the entire pipeline: how visitors arrive, how they're captured, how they're nurtured, how they convert, and how they return. These five stages are what we collectively call a revenue system.

The math is simple: a 1% conversion rate on 10,000 visitors is 100 customers. A 3% conversion rate on the same 10,000 visitors — with no extra ad spend — is 300 customers. That single improvement is worth 3x more revenue on the same traffic. A revenue system exists to make those improvements compounding, not one-off.

Custom software development, AI automation, WhatsApp Business API flows, local SEO, and CRM integration are the components. But they have to be architected together. A fast website with no follow-up is as leaky as a CRM with no inbound. A revenue system ties them together into one coherent engine.

For D2C brands, a revenue system means: high-conversion storefront + cart recovery + repeat purchase engine + SEO-driven organic traffic. For real estate, it's: direct lead capture + WhatsApp nurturing + virtual tours + local SEO dominance. For clinics: online booking + appointment reminders + patient retention + Google Maps visibility. The pattern is always the same — the specifics change by industry.

home.title: Why 95% of business websites fail at their only job.
home.summary: Most business websites are beautiful brochures that don't convert. Here are the 7 reasons they fail — and how a performance-first build fixes each one.
body: The first reason is speed. Google Core Web Vitals research shows that every 1 second of additional load time drops conversion by 7%. A 5-second site loses 35% of buyers before they see the offer. We build on Next.js with edge deployment, image optimization, and sub-second time-to-interactive targets. Every page passes Google PageSpeed out of the box.

The second is mobile-first design. 60% of Indian traffic is on a phone. If your buttons are too small, your checkout has desktop-only steps, or your forms break on iOS keyboard, you're losing more than half your market silently. We build for thumbs first — 48px touch targets, single-column checkouts, one-handed navigation flows.

The third is trust signals. Testimonials, reviews, credentials, case studies, certifications, and live-support chat are not decoration — they're conversion levers. A buyer who sees five recent 5-star reviews converts 270% better than one who sees none.

The fourth is SEO. Most websites are built without schema markup, canonical tags, hreflang, or a proper sitemap. That means they're invisible to Google on day one. We build every site with technical SEO baked in — your site is indexable and rankable the moment it goes live.

The fifth is follow-up. A website that captures a lead but doesn't follow up within 5 minutes converts 70% worse than one that does. WhatsApp automation, email drip sequences, and CRM tagging are how you close the gap.
The sixth is analytics. If you can't see what's working, you can't improve it. GA4, Search Console, heatmaps, and custom event tracking are table stakes.

The seventh is compounding. A website you ship and forget gets worse every month. A revenue system gets better every month — because we're tuning conversion, shipping new automation, and adding content.

home.title: The WhatsApp automation playbook every Indian business should run.
home.summary: WhatsApp Business API is the single highest-ROI tool for any Indian B2C business. Here are the 8 flows that pay for themselves in the first 30 days.
body: Flow 1 — Welcome sequence. Triggered when someone first contacts your business. Sends brand introduction, top products/services, and a clear next step. Open rate on WhatsApp is 98% vs 20% for email.
Flow 2 — Cart abandonment. Triggered 30 minutes after a cart is abandoned. Sends a reminder with product image, price, and a one-click checkout link. Recovers 20–30% of abandoned carts on average.
Flow 3 — Order confirmation + tracking. Automated order, dispatch, and delivery notifications. Eliminates 50%+ of 'where is my order' customer support tickets.
Flow 4 — Appointment reminders. For clinics, salons, and service businesses. A 24-hour reminder cuts no-show rates from ~30% to ~10%.
Flow 5 — Post-purchase care. Triggered 3–7 days after delivery. Asks for feedback, offers care tips, and sets up the repeat purchase. Doubles repeat purchase rate on average.
Flow 6 — Recall reminders. For clinics and coaching institutes. Automated 30/60/90-day check-ins bring back lost patients/students without manual effort.
Flow 7 — Broadcast campaigns. Compliant, segmented broadcasts for new launches, sales, and offers. Open rates 5x email.
Flow 8 — Lead routing. When a new lead comes in, auto-route to the right salesperson with full context. Cuts response time from hours to seconds.
All 8 flows run on the official WhatsApp Business API, fully compliant with Meta's policies. We build, deploy, and maintain them as part of AutoSell Engine.

home.title: The local SEO playbook for Indian businesses in 2026.
home.summary: If your customers search '[service] near me' or '[service] in [city]', local SEO is the highest-ROI marketing channel you're not fully using. Here's how to dominate it.
body: Step 1 — Claim and optimize your Google Business Profile. Photos, services, Q&A, opening hours, booking links, and regular posts. Most Indian businesses have a profile but never update it. Weekly updates signal activity to Google and improve ranking.

Step 2 — Local keyword research. Tools like Ahrefs, SEMrush, and Google Keyword Planner tell you exactly what people in your city are searching. For a dental clinic in Bangalore: 'best dentist Bangalore', 'root canal cost Bangalore', 'dentist near me HSR layout'. Target these with dedicated pages.

Step 3 — On-page SEO. Every page needs a unique title, meta description, H1, and semantic HTML. Schema markup (LocalBusiness, MedicalOrganization, RealEstateAgent) tells Google exactly what kind of business you are.
Step 4 — Location pages. One page per city or locality you serve. Each page with unique content — not duplicates. Include local landmarks, directions, parking info, and testimonials from that area.
Step 5 — Citations and backlinks. Listings on Justdial, Sulekha, IndiaMART, local directories, and industry-specific sites. Consistency of NAP (Name, Address, Phone) across all citations is critical.
Step 6 — Reviews. Automated review request flows after every transaction. Reply to every review — positive and negative. Google rewards active review management.

Step 7 — Content marketing. Blog posts targeting local intent queries: 'how to choose a dentist in Bangalore', 'what a root canal actually costs in Bangalore'. These rank fast and drive consistent organic traffic.
Step 8 — Technical SEO. Mobile-first, fast loading, HTTPS, clean URL structure, XML sitemap, robots.txt, canonical tags. Without these, nothing else matters.
Done right, local SEO delivers top 3 rankings for 5–10 high-intent keywords within 4–6 months — and those rankings compound. You stop paying for every visitor.

home.title: Custom ERP vs Tally vs Excel: which one does your SME actually need?
home.summary: Most Indian SMEs outgrow Excel but underpay for SAP. Here's the honest comparison — and when a custom ERP is the right call.
body: Excel is free. It's also the reason your business runs on 5 disconnected spreadsheets, has no single source of truth, and depends on one person who 'knows where everything is'. Excel is fine for a 2-person startup. It breaks at 20+ employees or ₹5Cr+ turnover.

Tally is the default Indian accounting software. It's great for compliance, GST filing, and year-end accounts. But it's not an ERP. It doesn't handle inventory flow, vendor management, or sales pipelines. It doesn't have a mobile app your field team can use. And customizing it is painful.

SAP and Oracle are enterprise ERPs. Great for ₹500Cr+ businesses. Cripplingly expensive and overkill for an SME. A typical SAP implementation runs ₹50L–₹5Cr and 6–18 months. Most SMEs abandon mid-way.

Zoho One, Odoo, and Microsoft Dynamics 365 are cloud ERPs. Cheaper than SAP. Rigid workflows that rarely fit Indian business realities — GST quirks, credit cycles, informal vendor relationships, mixed cash/credit transactions.

A custom ERP (like our OperateOS) is the right call when: you're ₹5Cr+ turnover, you have 20+ employees, you run on 5+ tools that don't talk to each other, and your business has workflows no off-the-shelf ERP handles. We build in 12–16 weeks, phased delivery, ₹3–15L total.

The ROI math: if your accountant spends 8 hours/week reconciling Excel + Tally + WhatsApp + vendor emails, and a custom ERP cuts that to 1 hour/week, you save 364 hours/year. At ₹500/hour, that's ₹1.82L/year just from accounting. Multiply by sales, procurement, HR, and inventory teams, and most custom ERPs pay back in 6–9 months.

about.title: Why we started Sanat Dynamo.
about.summary: Because too many good businesses were dying on the vine — not because their product was bad, but because their revenue infrastructure was broken.
body: We started Sanat Dynamo after spending years watching brilliant founders build great products and then hand them to agencies that delivered beautiful websites with zero conversion. The disconnect was infuriating. The founder had a real business problem; the agency delivered a deliverable. Nobody was on the hook for whether revenue actually went up.

The hypothesis was simple: what if an agency measured itself the same way its clients do — by the number on the P&L? Not by pixels shipped, not by hours billed, but by actual revenue impact, tracked dollar-for-dollar against a baseline.

We productized six specific systems that cover the 95% of cases we see. RevSite Pro for websites. AutoSell Engine for WhatsApp + CRM automation. LocalDom SEO for local search dominance. GlobalScale Suite for international markets. OperateOS for custom ERP. GrowthOS Retainer for ongoing compounding execution. Each one with fixed scope, fixed price, fixed outcomes.

Three years in, we've worked with 50+ businesses across five industries. Every engagement is measured the same way: did revenue go up, or costs come down, in the 90-day window after go-live? If yes, we kept working. If no, we'd rather be honest about it than stretch the engagement.
That's still our north star. A revenue-focused systems partner, not a typical agency.

about.title: How we hire (and why it matters to you).
about.summary: Small, senior, embedded. Every engineer on your project can independently ship production code, talk to your customers, and challenge your assumptions.
body: We hire for three traits in this order: judgment, communication, and craft. A technically brilliant engineer who can't explain trade-offs to a non-technical founder is a liability on our projects. We specifically don't hire juniors to bill them at senior rates — the team you meet on the sales call is the team building your system.

Every engineer we hire has shipped production systems to real users at previous companies. Median experience is 8 years. We cap the team at 12 people so we know every project, every client, every technical decision. Growth beyond that dilutes the quality we're known for.

Process-wise, we use direct Slack with the build team (no intermediary account managers), weekly demos, and written changelogs. You see what's being built in real time — no black-box cycles, no 'we'll update you next Friday'. This is how product companies work internally; we apply the same discipline to client engagements.

The tradeoff is we're more expensive per hour than a typical agency. The math still works: you get senior execution, faster decisions, fewer rework cycles, and a system that actually moves your numbers.

about.title: What we guarantee (and what we don't).
about.summary: We guarantee fixed scope, fixed price, fixed timeline, and a written scope document before kickoff. We don't guarantee specific revenue numbers — but we do guarantee we'll be honest about the math.
body: We guarantee a written scope document before any development starts. The document includes exact deliverables, milestones, timeline, success metrics, and commercials. If anything changes mid-project, we write a change order first. No surprise scope creep, no surprise invoices.

We guarantee fixed pricing on all one-time projects. The only reasons a project cost changes are: (a) you change the scope, or (b) we hit a blocker that was genuinely unknowable at kickoff (and even then, we absorb the first week of rework).

We guarantee a fixed go-live date on every engagement. If we miss it because of our fault, we work nights and weekends to catch up at no additional cost. We almost never miss — because we scope carefully at the start.

What we don't guarantee is a specific revenue number. We can project it from benchmark data, we can commit to hitting conversion, traffic, and automation targets — but we won't guarantee that your revenue will grow by exactly X%. Anyone who does is lying.
What we will do is measure everything honestly against the baseline, month over month, and tell you what's working and what isn't. If something isn't paying back, we'll say so and pivot.

services.title: How we price — and why it's higher than the agency average.
services.summary: We price on outcome, not hours. Here's exactly how we calculate a project quote, and why you get more for your money than a cheaper agency.
body: Traditional agencies bill hours. A junior at ₹1,200/hour spends 3 hours doing what a senior does in 20 minutes. The senior costs more per hour but costs less per outcome. Worse, the hour-billing model creates perverse incentives: the agency makes more money when things go slowly.

We price on outcome. Every project has a fixed scope and a fixed price. The price is calculated from: (a) the complexity of the system, (b) the senior engineering time required, (c) the go-live support period, and (d) a buffer for the one or two things that always surprise us.

RevSite Pro starts at ₹60K for a 7-page mobile-first site with basic SEO. It scales up to ₹1.5L for a 15-page site with advanced CMS, multi-language support, and custom integrations. The difference is scope, not inflation.

AutoSell Engine setup is ₹80K for 5 flows and a standard CRM integration. It scales to ₹2L for 8+ flows, custom workflow logic, and WhatsApp Business API provisioning. The monthly retainer (₹15–30K) covers flow maintenance, new flow development, and monthly optimization.

OperateOS starts at ₹3L for a 3-module ERP (inventory + orders + invoicing). It scales to ₹15L for a full suite with HR/payroll, CRM, mobile app, and multi-location support. Phased delivery means you start seeing ROI in month 1, not month 6.

GrowthOS Retainer is ₹60K–1.5L/month depending on scope. Typically includes 40 dev hours, 4 blog posts, WhatsApp flow maintenance, and monthly optimization. Fixed SLA, fixed deliverables, fixed price.

Why higher than a ₹15K website agency? Because we ship systems that actually move revenue. A ₹60K RevSite Pro delivering a 2% conversion lift on ₹50L monthly revenue is worth ₹12L/year. A ₹15K brochure website delivering 0 is worth ₹0. The math always works at our end of the market.

services.title: Which package should you start with?
services.summary: Most founders don't need all six systems. They need the one that pays back fastest for their specific situation. Here's how to decide.
body: If your biggest problem is 'I'm getting traffic but no conversions', start with RevSite Pro. Website rebuild pays back in 30–60 days when conversion goes from 0.6% to 2% on existing traffic.

If your biggest problem is 'leads are coming in but going cold', start with AutoSell Engine. WhatsApp automation + CRM follows up with every lead in 30 seconds instead of 3 days. ROI visible in week 2.

If your biggest problem is 'I'm paying too much per click on Google Ads', start with LocalDom SEO. 4–6 months of work gives you top 3 organic rankings for 5–10 keywords. Your cost per visitor drops 90% long-term.
If your biggest problem is 'I can't sell to international buyers', start with GlobalScale Suite. Multi-language, multi-currency, NRI-ready checkout. New market revenue stream in 90 days.

If your biggest problem is 'my business runs on Excel and tribal knowledge', start with OperateOS. Custom ERP with inventory, orders, invoicing, HR. Pays back in 6–9 months through operational efficiency.
If your biggest problem is 'I need a tech partner long-term, not just a project', start with GrowthOS Retainer. Embedded senior team, fixed monthly cost, compounding output.
Still not sure? Book a free Revenue Audit. We'll map your entire funnel and tell you exactly which system has the highest ROI for your specific business — no pitch, just diagnosis.

services.title: Realistic timeline expectations for every package.
services.summary: How long each system takes to ship, see results, and fully pay back. Honest numbers, not marketing promises.
body: RevSite Pro — 6–10 weeks from kickoff to go-live. Conversion improvements visible in week 1 post-launch. SEO improvements take 60–90 days to show in rankings, 4–6 months to show in organic traffic.
AutoSell Engine — 2–4 weeks setup, 2 weeks fine-tuning. Automation runs from day 1 of go-live. Cart recovery and appointment reminder ROI visible in week 2. Full optimization takes 60 days.
LocalDom SEO — 3 months minimum engagement. First ranking improvements in 30–60 days. Top 3 rankings for most target keywords in 4–6 months. Organic traffic compounds over 12+ months.
GlobalScale Suite — 8–12 weeks to launch. First international orders in month 1. Real market traction (consistent monthly revenue) in 3–6 months.
OperateOS — 12–16 weeks phased delivery. First module (usually inventory) goes live in week 4. Second module in week 8. Full system in week 16. Team training and adoption takes another 4 weeks.

GrowthOS Retainer — Starts delivering in week 1. Every month is a compounding improvement on the last. Most clients see meaningful revenue uplift by month 3, with continued improvements monthly after.
These are honest numbers. We've hit them consistently across 50+ engagements. A faster promise is usually a worse outcome.

industries.title: The complete D2C revenue playbook (from ₹10L to ₹1Cr monthly GMV).
industries.summary: Most D2C brands plateau around ₹30–50L monthly GMV because their unit economics break. Here's the full playbook to push past it.
body: Stage 1 — Fix the website. Most D2C brands on Shopify use a generic theme that converts at 0.6–1%. A rebuild with mobile-first UX, fast loading, trust signals, and a streamlined checkout can push conversion to 2–3% with no change in traffic. That single change can 2x revenue.

Stage 2 — Cut cart abandonment. Typical D2C brands see 75–80% cart abandonment. A 3-step WhatsApp + email recovery sequence recovers 20–30% of those carts. On ₹30L monthly revenue with 75% abandonment, that's ₹4.5–6L of recovered revenue per month.

Stage 3 — Build repeat purchase. First-time customers cost 5–7x what repeat customers cost. A post-purchase WhatsApp sequence (7 days + 30 days + 60 days) with care tips, offers, and new product intros doubles repeat purchase rate on most brands.

Stage 4 — Reduce ad dependence. At ₹30L monthly, you're probably spending ₹5–8L on Meta and Google ads. SEO content (4 blog posts/month targeting product-intent keywords) drives organic traffic over 6–12 months, reducing ad spend 30–50% while maintaining revenue.

Stage 5 — Go international. Once you're doing ₹50L+ monthly, NRIs and Southeast Asian customers are a massive untapped market. Multi-language, multi-currency, international payment gateway — launch a second revenue stream in 90 days.
Stage 6 — Add retention infrastructure. Loyalty programs, subscription boxes, referral engines. These are the mechanisms that turn ₹50L brands into ₹1Cr brands. Without them, you plateau.
The full playbook takes 9–12 months to execute. Most brands we work with go from ₹30–50L to ₹1Cr+ monthly GMV in that window, at the same or lower ad spend.

industries.title: How to stop depending on 99acres and housing.com.
industries.summary: Real estate developers pay ₹500–2,000 per low-intent portal lead. Here's how to build a direct lead pipeline that costs 60% less and converts 2x better.
body: Portal leads are inherently low-intent. Buyers on 99acres and housing.com are shopping 10+ properties simultaneously. They're comparison shopping, not buying. That's why your CPL is ₹500–2,000 and your conversion is 1–3%.

Direct leads from your own website are fundamentally different. They're searching for you specifically, or they're responding to a well-targeted ad that sent them to your landing page. They're 3–5x more likely to convert.

Step 1 — Build a proper real estate website. Not a brochure. A website with dynamic listings (synced to your CRM), real-time availability, filters by budget/location/configuration, and high-quality photos/videos/virtual tours.
Step 2 — WhatsApp lead capture on every listing page. Auto-response within 30 seconds. Agent assignment based on property. Follow-up drip sequence for 30 days.

Step 3 — CRM with automated nurturing. Track every lead through stages (new → contacted → site visit booked → visited → negotiation → closed). Automated reminders to agents when a lead hasn't been touched in 48 hours.
Step 4 — Virtual tours for NRI buyers. 360° walkthroughs, drone footage, video call scheduling. NRI deals are 2–3x larger than local deals and zero physical logistics required.
Step 5 — Local SEO dominance. Rank for '[city] flats for sale', '[locality] apartments', '3 BHK [city]'. Free leads compounding over time.

The end result is a pipeline where 60%+ of your leads come from your own channels, at 40% lower CPL, with 2x conversion to site visit and 3x conversion to deal. We've seen it work for 10+ Indian real estate developers.

industries.title: The automated admissions funnel for coaching institutes.
industries.summary: Most institutes lose 60–70% of inquiries because follow-up is manual. Here's how to build a self-serve enrollment system that converts 40% more.
body: The typical inquiry flow: parent calls → receptionist takes details → passes to counselor → counselor calls back 2 days later → parent has already joined a competitor. Conversion: 20–30% at best.

The automated flow: parent fills form on website → instant WhatsApp response → automated course info + fee structure → counselor notified within 60 seconds → counselor call within 2 hours → online fee payment link → instant enrollment confirmation. Conversion: 40–60%.

The difference is speed and consistency. Parents who are shopping coaching institutes typically call 3–5 places. Whoever responds first with clear information wins 60%+ of the time. A human counselor answering the phone can't match a 30-second automated response.
Step 1 — Build a fast, mobile-first website with clear course pages (fees, schedule, faculty, syllabus, testimonials). Parents decide on mobile in 90 seconds.
Step 2 — Integrate online fee payment (Razorpay/PayU). Parents who want instant enrollment are your highest-intent leads. Don't lose them to 'come to the office with cash'.
Step 3 — WhatsApp admission funnel. Auto-respond, capture details, send brochure PDF, schedule counselor call — all in under 2 minutes.
Step 4 — Automated follow-up for inquiries that don't convert immediately. Day 1, 3, 7, 14 touchpoints with different content (testimonials, batch starting soon, faculty introductions).
Step 5 — Student engagement after enrollment. Attendance tracking, assignment reminders, progress reports to parents. This reduces dropout by 30%+ — which directly compounds into referrals.
The full automated admissions + retention stack is built in AutoSell Engine + OperateOS. Most coaching institutes recoup the investment in one admission cycle.

caseStudies.title: How we track and verify every case study result.
caseStudies.summary: Every number in our case studies is tracked against a written baseline. Here's the exact methodology.
body: Step 1 — Baseline measurement. Before any engagement begins, we record the client's current metrics: monthly revenue, conversion rate, cost per acquisition, organic traffic, cart abandonment, support ticket volume, etc. These numbers come from the client's own tools (Shopify, Google Analytics, Meta Ads Manager, their CRM). We never estimate or back-calculate.

Step 2 — Success metrics in the scope document. Every project scope explicitly lists the metrics we're committing to move, with a baseline and a target for each. Example: 'Monthly revenue baseline ₹30L, target ₹45L by month 3'. This is agreed and signed before any code is written.
Step 3 — Monthly tracking. We pull fresh numbers monthly from the same tools. Month-over-month and cumulative deltas are reported transparently.

Step 4 — Case study numbers. When we publish a case study, the numbers come directly from the monthly tracking. We never round up, we never cherry-pick. If conversion went from 0.6% to 2.1%, we say 2.1%, not 'over 2x'.

Step 5 — Client review. Every case study is reviewed and approved by the client before publishing. They see the numbers we're quoting and the claims we're making, and sign off. If they want anonymization (because of competitive concerns), we anonymize — but the numbers stay accurate.

caseStudies.title: The engagements we don't put in case studies.
caseStudies.summary: About 15% of our engagements don't become case studies. Here's what happened — and why it's useful for you to know.
body: Some projects don't hit their targets in 90 days. Not because the work was bad, but because the underlying business had deeper issues — wrong product-market fit, founder disagreements, cash flow problems. In those cases, we were honest: we told the client the work wouldn't pay back in the planned timeline, and suggested they delay or cancel. Those conversations are harder than pitching, but they build the relationships that last.
Some clients have strict confidentiality requirements. Banks, healthcare groups, family-owned businesses that don't want visibility. We respect that fully — our NDAs work both ways.
Some outcomes are too early to publish. A 90-day revenue lift is real. A 6-month compounding trend is more meaningful. We wait until the story is fully told before writing it up.

If you're evaluating us and want to talk to a current client, we can usually arrange a reference call — including with clients whose stories aren't public. Honest references matter more than glossy case studies.

contact.title: What to bring to your Revenue Audit (nothing required).
contact.summary: You don't need to prepare anything to book an audit. But if you come with 5 minutes of data, we can go much deeper in the same 30 minutes.
body: Nothing is required — if you want to just walk in and talk, we can do that. But if you want us to show you specific, data-backed leaks in your funnel in the first call, bring these:
Your current monthly revenue (approximate is fine).
Your main traffic source (Meta Ads, Google Ads, SEO, referrals, WhatsApp broadcasts).
Your conversion rate if you know it (from Shopify, your website analytics, or your CRM).
Your top 1–2 business goals for the next 6 months (e.g., 'double monthly revenue', 'reduce ad spend 30%', 'automate customer support').
Your biggest operational frustration right now (e.g., 'my team spends 20 hours/week on manual WhatsApp follow-ups').

With these five data points, we can usually identify 2–3 specific revenue leaks in the first 15 minutes and show you exactly what it would take to fix them. The other 15 minutes we spend on questions from you. No slide deck, no pitch.

contact.title: What happens after you fill the form.
contact.summary: We reply within one business day, every time. Here's exactly what the first 7 days look like.
body: Day 0 (within a few hours) — Automated confirmation email. Your submission is logged and routed to the right strategist based on your industry.
Day 1 (within one business day) — Personal reply from a senior team member (not an account manager). We propose 2–3 specific 30-minute slots that week.
Day 2–3 — Revenue Audit call. 30 minutes, no pitch, just diagnosis. We ask questions, we identify leaks, we tell you exactly what it would take to fix them.
Day 4 (within 24 hours of the call) — Written summary of the audit. 2–4 pages. What we found, what we'd recommend, rough investment range, and the next step if you want to proceed.

Day 5–7 — You decide. No pressure, no follow-up-every-day harassment. If you want to move forward, the next step is a paid discovery session (3 hours, ₹15K, offset against any project). If you don't, we close the loop cleanly and you walk away with a free written audit.
